Details: Granular activated carbon is currently the best available technology (BAT) designated by US Environmental Protection Agency [1522]. Removal efficiency ranges from 85 to 95 percent depending upon design parameters [1520, 1521, 1522]. Removal of oxamyl by an activated carbon filter (Pentek model RFC20-BB) was reported as 99 percent in a lab water [1489].  The GAC filter tested was a component of a point-of-entry Hyd-RO-Secure drinking water treatment system. The GAC influent contained 210 μg/L oxamyl with the pH level of 7.2.
